_meta.method: Flywire-specific rule-based engine — corridor/impact/action from FLYWIRE_INTEL templates, urgency/confidence/ETA derived from change log trigger types and effective dates

predictions.title: AU surcharge: Surcharging permitted (cost-of-acceptance); no foreign-card cap → Surcharging banned from 1 Oct 2026; domestic interchange caps cut; foreign-card cap from 1 Apr 2027
predictions.impact: Flywire cannot pass card acceptance costs to payers at AU institutions via surcharge from 1 Oct 2026 (RBA final decision). Domestic interchange caps also cut from same date. Foreign-card interchange cap follows 1 Apr 2027 (cost saving for international student home-country cards).
predictions.action:
1. Audit all AU merchant agreements — identify where Flywire currently passes surcharges.
2. Model AU card acceptance costs under new lower interchange caps.
3. Decide: absorb into spread, increase base pricing, or negotiate acquirer rate reduction.
4. Notify AU institutional clients of any pricing changes before 1 Oct 2026.
predictions.evidence: No surcharging clarity until 2026 — SmartCompany (2025-10-24) → RBA Conclusions Paper — Merchant Card Payment Costs and Surcharging (2026-03-31)

predictions.title: US surcharge: No interchange cap; 4% surcharge cap (uniform required) → 125bps cap on qualifying consumer credit; 3% surcharge cap (uniform) or 1% (selective)
predictions.corridor: US cross-border CNP — education and healthcare payments
predictions.impact: Visa/Mastercard antitrust settlement changed US surcharge rules: 3% cap if applied uniformly across all card brands, 1% cap if applied selectively to Visa/MC only. Surcharging on debit/prepaid cards remains prohibited. Cart-level disclosure required.
predictions.action:
1. Review Flywire US surcharge model — confirm which cap applies based on current implementation.
2. Verify cart-level disclosure compliance (required since Feb 2025).
3. Confirm debit/prepaid cards are excluded from any surcharge.
predictions.evidence: Visa/Mastercard Interchange Settlement — Davis Wright Tremaine (2024-06-01)

predictions.title: UK PSR publishes market review into card-acquiring services
predictions.impact: Post-Brexit: EU-issued cards at UK merchants cost up to 1.50% credit / 1.20% debit CNP (was 0.30%/0.20% under EU IFR). Surcharging banned in UK (Consumer Rights Act 2018) so Flywire/institution absorbs the full cross-border cost. PSR market review may re-cap toward 0.30% — a 120bps saving on EU student flows to UK universities.
predictions.action:
1. Quantify total EU-issued card CNP volume at UK merchants over last 12 months.
2. Model the margin impact of a 0.30% re-cap scenario vs current 1.50%.
3. Track PSR card-acquiring market review — submit response if consultation opens.
4. Avoid locking UK institutional pricing contracts beyond Q2 2026 without a PSR review clause.
predictions.evidence: The Future of Interchange Fees: 2026 — Clearly Payments (2024-01-01) → UK Payment Systems Regulator — Card Acquiring Market Review (2024-03-01)

predictions.title: US interchange: 0.05%, 0.25%, 0.30%, 0.35% → 0.05%, 0.25%, 0.30%, 0.35%
predictions.corridor: US domestic payments — education, healthcare, travel (Visa/MC CNP)
predictions.impact: US has the highest CNP credit interchange globally (2.30–2.40% Visa Signature/Infinite). Reg II debit cap vacated by court (Aug 2025, stayed pending 8th Circuit appeal) — if upheld, US debit costs could rise 2–4x from current 21¢+5bps. Antitrust settlement caps qualifying consumer credit at 125bps from Mar 2025.
predictions.action:
1. Identify what percentage of US volume is domestic debit — model 2x/4x cost scenarios.
2. Verify whether Flywire's US institutional clients qualify for the 125bps antitrust cap.
3. Monitor Eighth Circuit case docket — ruling timing determines urgency of debit cost action.
4. Ensure US acquirer contracts allow interchange pass-through adjustment.
predictions.evidence: Visa USA Interchange Reimbursement Fees (2026-06-24) → Mastercard US Interchange Rates — Merchant Cost Consulting (2026-06-26)

predictions.title: EBA — Payment Services & Interchange updated
predictions.corridor: EU domestic and cross-border CNP — education and healthcare
predictions.impact: EU IFR caps consumer credit CNP at 0.30% (cheapest market globally for Flywire). Non-EEA cards (e.g. US/AU student paying EU university) cost 1.50% credit CNP — capped until Nov 2029 by voluntary Visa/MC commitment (confirmed Jul 2024).
predictions.action:
1. Low urgency — EU caps stable and extended to 2029.
2. Ensure EU acquirer contracts correctly apply IFR rates to consumer cards.
3. Flag commercial/corporate card volumes — these are NOT covered by IFR caps.
4. Monitor EU Parliament for proposed IFR debit cap reduction (0.20%→0.15%).
predictions.evidence: Visa and Mastercard extend fee caps on non-EU cards until 2029 — FStech (2024-07-08) → EBA — Payment Services & Interchange (2026-06-24)

predictions.title: CA surcharge: 30 days notice required to both Visa AND acquirer → 30 days notice to acquirer only (Visa notification no longer required)
predictions.corridor: CA domestic CNP — education payments (credit cards only; Quebec exempt)
predictions.impact: Credit card surcharging legal since Oct 2022 at 2.4% cap. Quebec institutions cannot surcharge (Consumer Protection Act). Debit/prepaid excluded.
predictions.action:
1. Verify Quebec institutions are excluded from Flywire's CA surcharge model.
2. Confirm debit/prepaid cards are not surcharged in Canada.
3. Ensure 30-day acquirer advance notice requirement is met before any surcharge changes.
predictions.evidence: Mastercard Canada — Merchant Surcharge Rules (2024-01-01) → Updates to Visa Surcharge Rules in Canada — Nuvei (2024-01-01)

predictions.title: FCAC — Annual Report & Payment Card News updated
predictions.impact: SMB interchange relief targets ~0.95% average effective credit interchange for eligible merchants. Visa SMB credit 1.35%, debit 0.50% (from Mar 2025). Flywire's CA institutional clients may qualify for SMB rates.
predictions.action:
1. Confirm which CA institutions qualify for Visa/MC SMB interchange rates.
2. Update CA pricing models to reflect Mar 2025 rate reduction (1.40%→1.35% credit).
3. Flag for annual CA pricing review.
predictions.evidence: The Future of Interchange Fees: 2026 — Clearly Payments (2024-10-01) → FCAC — Annual Report & Payment Card News (2026-06-26)

predictions.title: MAS — Payment Services Regulation updated
predictions.corridor: SG domestic and cross-border CNP — education and healthcare
predictions.impact: No government interchange caps in Singapore — rates set by Visa/Mastercard alone. MAS parliamentary scrutiny increasing (Mar 2026). Surcharging allowed at cost of acceptance; blanket brand surcharges banned Sep 2024.
predictions.action:
1. Monitor MAS consultation publications — no imminent rule change confirmed.
2. Ensure Flywire's SG surcharge model is cost-of-acceptance only (no blanket brand surcharges).
3. Pre-transaction disclosure required for any surcharge.
predictions.evidence: MAS — Payment Services Regulation (2026-06-25) → MAS — Payment Services Regulation (2026-06-25)

predictions.title: Japan FSA — Recent Releases (incl. Payment Services) updated
predictions.impact: Japan debit restructured Nov 2024: contactless NFC 0.90%, new QR tier 0.75%. JFTC/METI interchange disclosure requirement in force since Sep 2022. Cashless ratio reached 42.8% in 2024 (exceeded 40% target).
predictions.action:
1. Update JP pricing models to reflect Nov 2024 debit restructuring.
2. Monitor METI cashless policy for any further rate mandates.
3. Low urgency — no further Visa/MC JP rule changes confirmed since Jan 2024.
predictions.evidence: Payments Regulation in Asia — CMSPI (2024-01-01) → Japan FSA — Recent Releases (incl. Payment Services) (2026-06-26)
